summary: Exceptional soundboard remaster captures a high‑energy 1990 Chicago show with standout performances of Samson, Estimated Prophet and Brent Mydland’s Far From Me.
review: The July‑22‑1990 recording (soundboard) has been lovingly remastered by Jay Ashley, delivering crystal‑clear balance that fans repeatedly call “remarkable” and “gold”.  Musically the show is a tour‑de‑force – rare Box of Rain opener, soaring West L.A. Fadeaway, monster jams on Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World, and Brent Mydland stealing the spotlight on Hey Pocky Way and Far From Me – with only a few minor slip‑ups (missed verse in Hell in a Bucket, a beat‑count hiccup at the start of B.I.O.D.T.L.).

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer stars (mostly 4–5), enthusiastic language about both performance and remaster quality, with only minor performance quirks.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered by Jay Ashley; described as crisp, remarkable, gold‑standard. No reported dropouts or major flaws – the mix is clear and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A rare Box of Rain opener kicks off an electrified first set that builds through Loser and West L.A. Fadeaway, while the second set delivers seamless transitions – Samson into Hey Pocky Way, then a monster Estimated Prophet → Eyes jam, space‑wheel sequence, and a soaring close with Knockin on Heaven's Door.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring solos (West L.A. Fadeaway, Wheel, Knockin on Heaven’s Door) and tight rhythm work; occasional MIDI sax noted but effective.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted as wonderful throughout – strong bass on Samson, “philnonimal” intro to Gimme Some Lovin', emotive playing on Stella Blue and overall solid foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Strong vocal delivery overall; a brief beat‑count confusion at the start of B.I.O.D.T.L. and slightly rough harmonies before the bridge in Stella Blue were the only slips mentioned.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s performance described as top‑notch – commanding organ work, standout vocals on Far from Me, Hey Pocky Way and Knockin on Heaven's Door; his B3 solos praised.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as “awesome post drums”, short but punchy fills, classic cosmic space vibe, and tight support throughout the jams.
